content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 41: The prototype story

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to creativity and innovation: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
CREATION & DRAFTING | STEADY STATE | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Make an idea easier to evaluate by turning it into a concrete user story.

THE SITUATION:
Choose a product, service, process, or content idea. Make it tangible.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Make an idea easier to evaluate by turning it into a concrete user story. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to write a short story of one user encountering the idea.

2. Ask it to identify the moment of value, confusion, trust, and drop-off.

3. Rewrite the idea to strengthen the moment of value and reduce drop-off.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- The story has a real user and situation.
- Value and friction moments are visible.
- The idea improves after the story.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to product ideas, workshop design, content concepts, process improvements, naming, service design, and the useful art of making a problem interesting enough to solve.

COACHING NOTE:
People judge concrete stories better than abstract concepts. AI can help you get from "thing" to lived experience quickly.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Save the user story as prototype evidence. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
